description: Static database of providers that can PAY an arbitrary third-party BOLT11 Lightning invoice (paste/scan an invoice and send to it, including paste-invoice-as-withdrawal flows). Two wizard flows: (1) crypto — pick an asset, see country-independent routes in `crypto_routes`; (2) fiat — pick a rail (bank/card) then a country, see ranked providers in `fiat_rails`. All routes reference `providers` by id. Providers that only RECEIVE over Lightning are excluded and listed in `disqualified_providers`.
filter: pays_arbitrary_invoice == true — user can paste/scan a third-party BOLT11 invoice and the service pays/sends to it (inclusive of withdraw-to-pasted-invoice).

provider_fields.name: display name
provider_fields.mechanism: pay_invoice = explicit pay/send-to-invoice UX | withdraw_to_invoice = paste external invoice as a withdrawal/swap payout
provider_fields.lightning_docs_url: official 'how to pay/send to a Lightning invoice' page, or null if none found

rizful.name: Rizful.com
rizful.blurb: Pay Lightning invoices from an on-chain BTC balance. The cleanest way to get from on-chain BTC to Lightning.
rizful.caveat: Requires an email/password account. Sending needs Lightning balance; small routing fee.

boltz.name: Boltz
boltz.blurb: Atomic Lightning ↔ USDT swaps (via tBTC + USDT0 on Arbitrum). ~0.1% service fee plus DEX and miner fees.

strike.name: Strike
strike.blurb: Pays BOLT11 invoices directly from a USD (or USDT in EU markets) cash balance using Lightning as internal settlement. ~0.15% routing fee, available in 65+ countries.

aqua.name: Aqua Wallet
aqua.blurb: Mobile wallet. Holds on-chain BTC, Liquid BTC, and USDT-Liquid; pays any pasted BOLT11 invoice via internal Boltz swaps.

phoenix.name: Phoenix
phoenix.blurb: Lightning wallet with splicing for on-chain ↔ Lightning conversion.
phoenix.caveat: Must fund wallet first. ~0.4% fee on outgoing Lightning payments (min 4 sats).

zeus.name: Zeus (+ ZEUS Swaps)
zeus.blurb: LN wallet; v0.11 added ZEUS Swaps for bidirectional on-chain ↔ Lightning moves.

coinbase.name: Coinbase (Lightspark)
coinbase.blurb: Trade your altcoin to BTC, then withdraw over Lightning (~0.1% send fee). ~15% of Coinbase BTC withdrawals now ride Lightning.
coinbase.caveat: Accepts arbitrary external invoices (invoices only, NOT Lightning addresses). Excludes New York and Canada; ~0.2% processing fee; rolling out by region.

kraken.name: Kraken
kraken.blurb: Runs its own LND node; verified accounts can deposit and withdraw BTC via Lightning.
kraken.caveat: Generate invoice in external wallet, paste into Kraken withdrawal. Verified account required. Not available in Singapore.

binance.name: Binance
binance.blurb: Up to 100 BTC/day Lightning withdrawal for verified accounts; minimum 0.00001 BTC. Not available on Binance.US.
binance.caveat: Withdraw → BTC → Lightning → paste invoice. Not available to US users; Lightning may be unavailable in some regions due to local regulation.

okx.name: OKX
okx.blurb: Oldest CEX Lightning integration (April 2021). Not available to US users.

bitfinex.name: Bitfinex
bitfinex.blurb: LN-BTC token with dynamic withdrawal fee. Zero trading fees since Dec 2025.

kucoin.name: KuCoin
kucoin.blurb: Explicit Lightning option in web / app UI for BTC withdrawals.
kucoin.caveat: Qualifies (LN withdrawal to pasted invoice) but a specific step-by-step docs page could not be verified. Requires holding BTC.

cashapp.name: Cash App
cashapp.blurb: US-only. Send/receive BOLT11 and pay any Lightning Address via $cashtag.
cashapp.caveat: Excluded in New York. Up to $999 send/receive over Lightning per 7 days.

river.name: River Financial
river.blurb: Bitcoin-only US exchange. Up to 0.01 BTC per Lightning withdrawal.
river.caveat: US-only. Enter a Lightning invoice, LNURL-Pay, or Lightning address; up to 0.1 BTC, one free send/month.

coincorner.name: CoinCorner
coincorner.blurb: UK / Isle of Man focus. Lightning Addresses name@coincorner.io, Bolt Card support.

bullbitcoin.name: Bull Bitcoin
bullbitcoin.blurb: Canada / EU Lightning withdrawals; Instant Payments via Liquid + Boltz.

simpleswap.name: SimpleSwap
simpleswap.blurb: Global swap service; additional review may apply on some transfers.
simpleswap.caveat: Swap INTO BTC-LN: supply your own external invoice as payout. LN invoice valid ~1 hour.

changenow.name: ChangeNOW
changenow.blurb: Swap almost any asset into Bitcoin (Lightning Network); you supply an external BOLT11 invoice as the payout destination.
changenow.caveat: Reclassified as qualifying (was receive-only). No dedicated how-to page; mechanism inferred from BTC-LN being a payout asset.

fixedfloat.name: FixedFloat
fixedfloat.blurb: Most LN-native swap service: runs its own Lightning node, fixed / float rates, 0.5–1% fee. TOS prohibits US users.
fixedfloat.caveat: Swap any coin → BTC-LN; paste your Lightning invoice as payout. US persons cannot use FixedFloat; routing may fail with no open channel.

sideshift.name: SideShift.ai
sideshift.blurb: Swap service with Lightning as output. Max ≤0.04 BTC per shift.
sideshift.caveat: Swap to BTC-Lightning; supply your invoice as payout. Blocked in US and sanctioned jurisdictions; no account required. No official how-to page found.

blink.name: Blink
blink.blurb: El Salvador (formerly Bitcoin Beach Wallet). In-app buy/sell via local bank transfer; full Lightning send/receive, LNURL, POS.
blink.caveat: Buy/sell requires a Salvadoran bank account; wallet is global.

pouch.name: Pouch
pouch.blurb: Philippines. Lightning wallet funded by InstaPay/PESONet/QR-PH; pay via invoice or Lightning address.

bitnob.name: Bitnob
bitnob.blurb: Nigeria/Ghana/Kenya and West Africa. Local-currency deposits; send BTC to Lightning address or invoice. Powers Strike Send Globally to Africa.

bringin.name: Bringin
bringin.blurb: Eurozone/EEA. Lightning wallet with dedicated vIBAN + debit card; sell BTC over Lightning for instant SEPA payout.
bringin.caveat: Send → Pay Lightning Invoice → paste → approve. EU/EEA.

mtpelerin.name: Mt Pelerin
mtpelerin.blurb: Switzerland/EU (SEPA, Swiss, SWIFT) + card. Buy Lightning sats delivered to any wallet; sell from Lightning to bank.
mtpelerin.caveat: Bridge Wallet app natively pays arbitrary invoices. Swiss-regulated; availability and limits vary.

bipa.name: Bipa
bipa.blurb: Brazil. PIX deposits + card with sats cashback; send by BOLT11 invoice or Lightning address. First Brazil app with Lightning.

bitso.name: Bitso
bitso.blurb: Mexico/Argentina/Colombia/Brazil. Local bank rails (SPEI, PIX, etc.); Lightspark-powered Lightning send/receive, min 1 sat.

ripio.name: Ripio
ripio.blurb: Argentina/Brazil + LatAm. Bank transfer + Visa crypto card; Lightning withdrawal, Lightning Address (tag@ripio.com), BOLT11/LNURL.

belo.name: Belo
belo.blurb: Argentina. Bank transfer (ARS) + Mastercard prepaid; OpenNode-powered, withdraw BTC via Lightning free.

shakepay.name: Shakepay
shakepay.blurb: Canada. Interac e-Transfer / wire; send via Lightning invoice or LNURL. First BTC withdrawal each month free.

bity.name: Bity / Bitybank
bity.blurb: Brazil. PIX deposits; Lightning withdrawal/deposit with zero withdrawal fee.

chivo.name: Chivo
chivo.blurb: El Salvador government wallet. Bank/ATM cash; send/receive over Lightning (River-powered).
chivo.caveat: Reliability/security problems reported; Bitcoin legal-tender mandate rolled back.

speed.name: Speed
speed.blurb: India-operated Lightning-native wallet; buy and pay over Lightning.
speed.caveat: Regulatory uncertainty in India; verify availability.

walletofsatoshi.name: Wallet of Satoshi
walletofsatoshi.blurb: Lightning wallet. Scan/paste an invoice, LNURL, or Lightning address to send. Funded by receiving BTC/LN in — no built-in fiat ramp.

muun.name: Muun
muun.blurb: Wallet that scans/pays any Lightning invoice via submarine swap from on-chain BTC. Funded by crypto-in; incurs on-chain swap fee.
muun.caveat: Wallet only — no fiat purchase path. Best when you already hold on-chain BTC.

crypto_routes.label: Lightning
crypto_routes.summary: You're already on Lightning. Scan the QR code above with any LN wallet or paste the BOLT11 invoice into it.

crypto_routes.label: Bitcoin (on-chain)
crypto_routes.summary: Rizful pays Lightning invoices directly from on-chain BTC — no account needed beyond a quick signup. Swap services and exchanges can also pay an invoice you paste, if you already hold BTC.

providers.blurb_override: Lightning ↔ on-chain BTC swap — paste the invoice and send BTC to the swap address.

crypto_routes.label: Tether (USDT — any chain)
crypto_routes.summary: Swap USDT to a Lightning invoice through a swap service.

crypto_routes.label: USD Coin (USDC)
crypto_routes.summary: Swap USDC to a Lightning invoice via a swap service, or withdraw from a CEX to your invoice.

crypto_routes.label: Monero (XMR)
crypto_routes.summary: Route via a privacy-respecting swap service, ideally over Tor.

crypto_routes.label: Dogecoin (DOGE)
crypto_routes.summary: Swap DOGE to a Lightning invoice.

crypto_routes.label: Tron (TRX)
crypto_routes.summary: Swap TRX directly to a Lightning invoice.

crypto_routes.label: Solana (SOL)
crypto_routes.summary: Swap SOL to a Lightning invoice, or trade to BTC on a CEX and withdraw to your invoice.

crypto_routes.label: XRP
crypto_routes.summary: Swap XRP to a Lightning invoice.

crypto_routes.label: BNB
crypto_routes.summary: Swap BNB to a Lightning invoice, or withdraw from Binance to your invoice (non-US).

disqualified_providers.name: Relai
disqualified_providers.reason: Lightning wallet sunset — Relai is removing its Lightning wallet; users told to move funds/close channels.

disqualified_providers.name: Bitrefill
disqualified_providers.reason: Lightning is only an inbound payment method for Bitrefill's own products; no pay-arbitrary-invoice feature.

disqualified_providers.name: Pocket Bitcoin
disqualified_providers.reason: Receive-only — fiat→BTC is delivered to your own Lightning wallet; cannot pay a third-party invoice.

disqualified_providers.name: RoboSats
disqualified_providers.reason: Receive-only — you receive trade proceeds to your own invoice / pay only RoboSats' own escrow invoices.

disqualified_providers.name: Lemon Cash
disqualified_providers.reason: Lightning documented for receiving/deposits only; no verified flow to pay an arbitrary external invoice.

disqualified_providers.name: Peach Bitcoin
disqualified_providers.reason: P2P with on-chain multisig escrow; Lightning not live for paying arbitrary invoices.

disqualified_providers.name: Orangefren
disqualified_providers.reason: Could not verify arbitrary-invoice payout from any official documentation; excluded pending verification.
